channel.title: Nika · Notes from the source
channel.description: Long-form pedagogy on Intent as Code: why useful AI work belongs in a file, why the language locks at four verbs, and what local-first actually buys you.

item.title: Intent as Code: why your AI work should be a file
item.description: Useful AI work shouldn't disappear into chats. Nika's bet is simple: useful AI work is worth writing down. Not as a transcript, as source: a small YAML file that says what you want. The file is the workflow; run it again tomorrow and it does the same thing, and git diff shows exactly what changed.

item.title: Four verbs are enough
item.description: Every workflow language faces the same temptation: keep adding verbs. Nika locks at four (infer, exec, invoke, agent) because a verb is a distinct native execution model, and everything callable is a tool. Why the small language stays reviewable.
